description_long: This is a classic biplane with a double-wing configuration, featuring a predominantly white fuselage accented by bold red stripes on the upper wing and yellow and blue bands on the tail and fuselage. The nose is rounded and painted in a tan color, with a small propeller at the front and a visible landing gear beneath the lower wing. The cockpit is situated just behind the upper wing, covered by a transparent canopy, and appears to be a single-seat design. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all with matching color accents. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century stunt or trainer aircraft, with fixed landing gear and exposed struts connecting the wings. The surface finish is smooth and glossy, suggesting a metal or composite construction typical of small aircraft.
description: A white biplane with red stripes, yellow and blue accents, a tan nose, and a single-seat cockpit under a clear canopy. It features double wings, fixed landing gear, and a classic vintage design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double wings, red stripes, rounded tan nose, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the cockpit canopy, wing struts, and tail with yellow and blue bands.
description_view_2: Rear-front view focusing on the propeller, landing gear, and the symmetry of the biplane's wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view showing the cockpit, wing structure, and tail details from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, single-seat cockpit with an enclosed transparent canopy just behind the upper wing.
